title: verbascum thapsus subsp. litigiosum
intro: Verbascum thapsus subsp. litigiosum, also known as the mullein plant, is a perennial plant in the figwort family native to Europe. The medicinal properties of this plant include its mucilaginous leaves and flowers, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, making it a popular remedy for respiratory issues and skin irritations. The health benefits of Verbascum thapsus subsp. litigiosum include its ability to treat coughs, colds, and sore throats, as well as its anti-itching and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, salves, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
